preamble: The retraction of the voiceless postalveolar fricative [ʃ] to the voiceless retroflex fricative [ʂ]. This process of 'hardening' was a major historical shift in several Slavic languages.

examples.note: The hardening of historical soft sibilants into retroflexes.
The transition from a palatalized sibilant to a 'hard' retroflex fricative.

sources: Shevelov, G. Y. (1964). A Prehistory of Slavic.
Carlton, T. R. (1991). Introduction to the Phonological History of the Slavic Languages.
